scheme_name: PMMSY: Ice Plant/Cold Store of minimum 10-ton capacity Haryana
page_title: PMMSY: Ice Plant/Cold Store of minimum 10-ton capacity Haryana

details: The Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana: Ice Plant/Cold Store of minimum 10-ton capacity, introduced by the Ministry Of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying, Government of India, aims to provide the subsidy to support the establishment of ice plants or cold storage facilities with a minimum capacity of 10 tons to improve fish preservation and cold chain infrastructure in Haryana.

This centrally sponsored scheme is jointly funded 50% by the Central Government and 50% by the State Government. Eligible beneficiaries must undertake to keep the unit operational, cover maintenance and post-construction costs, and ensure ice is supplied to fishers and fish farmers at affordable prices.
benefits: A 40% subsidy is available for the General Category.
A 60% subsidy is offered for SC (Scheduled Caste) and Women beneficiaries.
eligibility: The applicant must be a resident of Haryana and possess a valid Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP).
The applicant must submit an undertaking in the DPR agreeing to bear all operational, maintenance, and post-construction costs.
The applicant must ensure that the ice plant/cold storage remains continuously operational.
The applicant must ensure that the ice produced is supplied to fishers and fish farmers at affordable prices.
The applicant must either own the land or have it on a long-term registered lease of at least 10 years.
The applicant must note that central assistance will be provided based on actual costs, within the overall ceiling limits specified under PMMSY sub-components.
The applicant must understand that, upon approval, the subsidy will be disbursed as per the procedure laid out under PMMSY.

online: For registration, click on “New User/Register Here” and fill in all the mandatory details,i.e., Name, Email ID, Mobile Number & password. Click on ‘Submit’.
Verify the OTP received in the provided Email and Mobile Number. Alternatively, click on the Verification Link to activate the profile.
Login to Apply for the Scheme:
The “Sign in here” option is available on the right side of the screen. Fill in the required credentials and click on the ‘Login’ button.
In the opened window, click on “Scheme/Services list”, and a list of schemes will appear on the screen.
Now, select the scheme and click on “Apply for Service/Scheme”.
Fill in all the details in the online application form and click on "Submit".
Type 'SARAL' and send it to 9954699899 to track your application.
Type SARAL<space><Application ID/Ticket No.> and send to 9954699899 to track your application/ticket.

documents_required: Date of Birth Certificate (Voter ID/Birth Certificate/Aadhaar/Matriculation Certificate)
Caste Certificate from First Class Magistrate (Tehsildar/SDM)
Agreement Deed between beneficiary and Fisheries Department
Training Certificate related to the operation/maintenance of cold storage
Land Record from Tehsil (Jamabandi, Nakal Akshizra, Fard, Registered Patta Nama) with clear title and latest records
Photographs of Cold Storage with beneficiary
Detail of Bank Account and PAN Card
Detailed Project Report (DPR) or Self-Contained Proposal (SCP) as per PMMSY guidelines
All bills above ₹50,000/- as per E-way/Income Tax/GST rules
Certificate from C.A. with the bills certifying payment compliance with Income Tax/GST rules
NOC from Gram Sabha/Municipal Corporation/Authority of the area where the unit is located
Clear Site Plan/Area Site Plan attached with subsidy case
All documents should be in readable form

faqs.question: Who can apply for the Ice Plant/Cold Storage subsidy under PMMSY?
faqs.answer: Any beneficiary having a valid Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP) is eligible to apply for this scheme.

faqs.question: Can I apply if I have land on lease instead of owning it?
faqs.answer: Yes, land on long-term registered lease is acceptable if the lease period is at least 10 years and the land title is clear.

faqs.question: Is it mandatory to keep the cold storage functional after construction?
faqs.answer: Yes, you must ensure the cold storage remains operational and bear all operational, maintenance, and post-construction costs.

faqs.question: Am I required to sell the ice at a fixed rate?
faqs.answer: Yes, the ice produced from the subsidized plant must be provided to fishers and fish farmers at an affordable price.

faqs.question: Can I submit a Self Contained Proposal instead of a DPR?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can submit either a Detailed Project Report (DPR) or a Self Contained Proposal (SCP) as per PMMSY guidelines.

faqs.question: What training certificate is required for this scheme?
faqs.answer: You must submit a training certificate related to the operation or maintenance of cold storage.

faqs.question: Do I need an agreement with the department?
faqs.answer: Yes, an agreement deed between you and the Fisheries Department is required for the application.

faqs.question: What kind of financial documentation is required?
faqs.answer: You must attach GST-compliant bills and a certificate from a Chartered Accountant confirming compliance with Income Tax and GST rules.

faqs.question: What other supporting documents do I need?
faqs.answer: You must attach a site plan, photographs of the cold storage with yourself, and ensure all documents are in a readable format.

faqs.question: Who can I contact for help during the application process?
faqs.answer: You can call the helpline at 0172-3968400 or email saral.haryana@gov.in for assistance.
